meta.title: Bangkok Guide
meta.subtitle: 21 must-visit places near Four Seasons Bangkok

accommodation.name: Four Seasons Hotel Bangkok
accommodation.description: Riverside luxury hotel on the Chao Phraya River with private boat shuttle
accommodation.tips: Free shuttle boat to BTS Saphan Taksin. Multiple restaurants on-site. Beautiful riverside pool and spa. Request a river-view room.

places.name: Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn)
places.description: Stunning riverside temple with iconic spire covered in colourful porcelain
places.tips: Go at sunrise or sunset for best photos. Cross the river by boat. Dress modestly.

places.name: Wat Pho (Reclining Buddha)
places.description: 46-metre gold reclining Buddha and Thailand's oldest traditional massage school
places.tips: Get a traditional Thai massage here ($10-15 for 30 min). Go early to beat crowds.

places.name: Grand Palace
places.description: Dazzling royal palace complex with the sacred Emerald Buddha
places.tips: Strict dress code (long pants, covered shoulders). Go at 8:30am opening. No shorts!

places.name: Wat Saket (Golden Mount)
places.description: Hilltop temple with 360-degree panoramic views of old Bangkok
places.tips: Climb 344 steps. Best at sunset. Less crowded than the big-name temples.

places.name: Jay Fai
places.description: Legendary street food stall with a Michelin star - famous for crab omelette
places.tips: Queue early (before 2pm) or you won't get in. Cash only.

places.name: Nahm (COMO Metropolitan)
places.description: World-renowned Thai fine dining - complex, authentic royal Thai cuisine
places.tips: Reserve ahead. Try the set menu for full range of flavours.

places.name: Pad Thai Thip Samai
places.description: Bangkok's most famous pad thai - cooked in a wok wrapped in egg
places.tips: Order the "superb" pad thai wrapped in egg. Open evenings only (5pm-2am).

places.name: Or Tor Kor Market
places.description: Bangkok's finest fresh market - tropical fruits, prepared Thai food
places.tips: Try mango sticky rice, som tam, seasonal fruits. Near Chatuchak.

places.name: Baan Ice
places.description: Home-style southern Thai food - fiery, bold, deeply flavourful
places.tips: Try the crab curry and dry curry. Very spicy! Michelin Bib Gourmand.

places.name: Charoen Saeng Silom
places.description: Legendary chicken rice (khao man gai) - Bangkok's best comfort food
places.tips: Simple but perfect. Chicken rice with soup on the side. Under $2.

places.name: Chatuchak Weekend Market
places.description: World's largest outdoor market - 15,000+ stalls over 35 acres
places.tips: Saturday & Sunday only. Go early (9am) to beat heat. Bring cash.

places.name: Asiatique The Riverfront
places.description: Night market and entertainment complex on the river with Ferris wheel
places.tips: Free shuttle boat from Saphan Taksin BTS. Opens 4pm.

places.name: Chinatown (Yaowarat Road)
places.description: Vibrant Chinatown that transforms into a street food paradise at night
places.tips: Best after 6pm. Try grilled seafood, bird's nest soup, mango sticky rice.

places.name: Floating Markets (Damnoen Saduak)
places.description: Iconic floating market where vendors sell from wooden boats
places.tips: Leave Bangkok by 7am (1.5hr drive). Combine with Maeklong Railway Market.

places.name: Maeklong Railway Market
places.description: Market on active train tracks - vendors pull back awnings as train passes!
places.tips: Train comes ~4 times daily. Check schedule.

places.name: Ayutthaya Historical Park
places.description: UNESCO World Heritage ruins of the ancient Siamese capital
places.tips: Train (1.5hr) or hire a driver. Rent a bicycle to explore. Full day.

places.name: Jim Thompson House
places.description: Teak house museum of the American silk king who mysteriously vanished
places.tips: Guided tours only (every 20 min). Great gift shop for Thai silk.

places.name: Sky Bar (Lebua State Tower)
places.description: Famous rooftop bar from "The Hangover Part II" - 63 floors up
places.tips: Smart casual (no shorts/sandals). Go for sunset. No reservations needed.

places.name: Bamboo Bar (Mandarin Oriental)
places.description: Legendary jazz bar since 1953 - one of Asia's most iconic bars
places.tips: Live jazz nightly. Smart casual. Reservations recommended on weekends.

places.name: Wat Pho Thai Massage
places.description: The birthplace of traditional Thai massage - authentic and affordable
places.tips: Book the 1-hour Thai massage ($10-15). Inside Wat Pho temple complex.

places.name: Chao Phraya River Boat
places.description: Explore Bangkok by boat along the "River of Kings"
places.tips: Public Express Boat ($0.50) or hire private longtail. Hotel has shuttle boat.
